title: rubus selmeri var. argyriophyllus
intro: Rubus selmeri var. argyriophyllus, also known as Arctic Raspberry or Mountain Raspberry, is a rare and endangered species of wild raspberry native to the Arctic tundra regions of Norway and Svalbard. Traditionally used in folk medicine, the plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Rubus selmeri var. argyriophyllus include its potential to prevent the growth of certain bacteria and viruses, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits and potential applications.
